US: US1: Save and quickly insert predefined responses in chats
AC: AC1: Add Saved Response button in Editor nav
Step:
1. Login as Coach
2. Open a conversation
3. Observe the Editor nav
4. Verify Saved Responses button
Title: Button position next to GIF button
Expectation: - SHOULD display a Saved Responses button next to the GIF button in Editor nav

Step:
1. Hover over the Saved Responses button
2. Verify tooltip
Title: Tooltip on hover
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip: "Saved Responses"

Step:
1. Click the Saved Responses button
2. Verify modal
Title: Modal opens on button click
Expectation: - SHOULD display Saved Responses modal

Step:
1. Press Ctrl/Cmd+K shortcut
2. Verify modal
Title: Modal opens on shortcut
Expectation: - SHOULD display Saved Responses modal

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Verify modal elements
Title: Modal contains X button, Search box, + Create new button
Expectation:
- SHOULD contain X button
- SHOULD contain Search box
- SHOULD contain + Create New button in top-right corner

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal (no saved responses)
2. Verify empty state
Title: Empty state display
Expectation:
- SHOULD display an image
- SHOULD display text: "Your saved responses list is empty. Start by creating your first one to save time during chats."

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal (has saved responses)
2. Verify FREQUENTLY USED section
Title: FREQUENTLY USED section hidden when none
Expectation: - SHOULD hide FREQUENTLY USED section when user has no usage history

Title: FREQUENTLY USED shows top 5 most-used
Expectation:
- SHOULD show up to top 5 most-used responses, sorted by usage_count descending
- SHOULD sort by title A-Z when usage_count is equal

Title: FREQUENTLY USED shows all when fewer than 5
Expectation: - SHOULD show all available responses when fewer than 5 exist

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal (has saved responses)
2. Verify ALL section
Title: ALL section lists responses A-Z
Expectation: - SHOULD list all saved responses sorted by title A-Z

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal (has saved responses)
2. Verify default selection
Title: First saved response selected by default
Expectation:
- SHOULD select first saved response in list by default
- SHOULD show its title and message detail on the right-side panel

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Press Up/Down arrow keys
3. Verify navigation
Title: Arrow-key navigation between responses
Expectation: - SHOULD move selection Up/Down between responses in the list

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal (has saved responses)
2. Hover over a response title in the list
3. Verify right panel
Title: Hover title shows message detail
Expectation: - SHOULD update the right-side panel to show the hovered response's title and message detail

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Observe right-side panel for a long message
3. Verify scrolling
Title: Long message scrollable
Expectation: - SHOULD allow scrolling when the message detail is long

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Hover over the message detail on right panel
3. Verify 3-dot button
Title: 3-dot button on message detail hover
Expectation: - SHOULD show 3-dot button when hovering over the message detail

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal (has >20 saved responses)
2. Scroll down in the list
3. Verify pagination
Title: Pagination loads 20 items on scroll
Expectation:
- SHOULD load 20 items initially
- SHOULD display Load more and load next 20 items on scroll

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Observe footer
Title: Footer shortcuts displayed
Expectation: - SHOULD show shortcuts: "↑↓ to navigate", "↵ to select", "Esc to close"

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click X button / press Esc
3. Verify modal
Title: X button / Esc closes modal
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Saved Responses modal

Step:
1. Hover over + Create New button
2. Verify display
Title: + Create New button hover display
Expectation: - SHOULD display as "+ Create New" on hover

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Observe Search box
3. Verify default state
Title: Search box default state
Expectation: - SHOULD be empty with placeholder: "Search saved response"

Step:
1. Click / focus the Search box
2. Verify placeholder
Title: Placeholder changes on focus
Expectation: - SHOULD change placeholder to "Enter keyword..."

Step:
1. Type text in Search box
2. Verify X icon
Title: X icon shown while typing
Expectation: - SHOULD display X icon when text is inputted

Step:
1. Type text in Search box
2. Clear via backspace or click X icon
3. Verify list
Title: Clear search restores full list
Expectation: - SHOULD remove search input and show the full saved response list

Step:
1. Type 1 character in Search box
2. Verify search triggers
Title: Search starts from 1 character
Expectation: - SHOULD start searching and return results from 1 character input

Step:
1. Search for a keyword matching title or message (different case / partial)
2. Verify results
Title: Case-insensitive partial search on title and message
Expectation: - SHOULD return all responses where title or message contains the keyword (case-insensitive, partial match)

Step:
1. Search for a keyword with no matching results
2. Verify empty results state
Title: No results state
Expectation:
- SHOULD display an image
- SHOULD display text: "Search results (0)"

Step:
1. Search for a keyword with matching results
2. Verify results display
Title: Matching results count and list
Expectation:
- SHOULD show "Search results ({number_of_matched_results})" below Search box
- SHOULD show matching responses list same as AC2
- SHOULD select first result by default

Step:
1. Search with >20 matching results
2. Scroll down
3. Verify pagination
Title: Pagination in search results
Expectation:
- SHOULD load 20 items initially
- SHOULD display Load more and load next 20 items on scroll

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Hover over a saved response message detail
3. Click 3-dot button
4. Verify submenu
Title: 3-dot submenu options
Expectation: - SHOULD show submenu with options: Edit response, Delete response

AC: AC5: Add a new saved response
Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click + Create New button
3. Verify popup
Title: Create Saved Response popup opens
Expectation: - SHOULD display Create Saved Response popup with title "Create Saved Response"

Step:
1. Open Create Saved Response popup
2. Verify popup elements
Title: Popup contains X, TITLE field, MESSAGE field, Cancel, Save
Expectation:
- SHOULD contain [X] button
- SHOULD contain TITLE field
- SHOULD contain MESSAGE field
- SHOULD contain Cancel button (always enabled)
- SHOULD contain Save button (disabled by default)

Step:
1. Hover over (info) icon next to TITLE field
2. Verify tooltip
Title: TITLE info icon tooltip
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip: "Used to organize responses. This won't appear in messages.."

Step:
1. Observe TITLE field
2. Verify default state
Title: TITLE field default state
Expectation: - SHOULD be empty with placeholder: "Name this response for easy search"

Step:
1. Type 140 characters in TITLE field
2. Try to type more
3. Verify field
Title: TITLE max 140 characters enforced
Expectation: - SHOULD not allow input beyond 140 characters

Step:
1. Paste text longer than 140 characters into TITLE field
2. Verify field content
Title: TITLE paste truncates to 140 characters
Expectation: - SHOULD paste only the first 140 characters

Step:
1. Observe MESSAGE field
2. Verify default state
Title: MESSAGE field default state
Expectation: - SHOULD be empty with placeholder: "Write the full message you want to send to clients"

Step:
1. Type in MESSAGE field
2. Verify character count
Title: MESSAGE character count shown
Expectation: - SHOULD show character count as "{X}/10000"

Step:
1. Type 10,000 characters in MESSAGE field
2. Try to type more
3. Verify field
Title: MESSAGE max 10,000 characters enforced
Expectation: - SHOULD not allow input beyond 10,000 characters

Step:
1. Paste text longer than 10,000 characters into MESSAGE field
2. Verify field content
Title: MESSAGE paste truncates to 10,000 characters
Expectation: - SHOULD paste only the first 10,000 characters

Step:
1. Paste text with formatting into MESSAGE field
2. Verify content
Title: MESSAGE clears formatting on paste
Expectation: - SHOULD strip text formatting from pasted text

Step:
1. Leave TITLE or MESSAGE field empty
2. Verify Save button state
Title: Save button disabled when fields incomplete
Expectation: - SHOULD keep Save button disabled when TITLE or MESSAGE is empty

Step:
1. Fill both TITLE and MESSAGE fields
2. Verify Save button state
Title: Save button enabled when both fields filled
Expectation: - SHOULD enable Save button when both TITLE and MESSAGE have input

Step:
1. Fill both fields
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save creates new saved response with toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD add new saved response to the list
- SHOULD display toast: "New Saved Response created."

Step:
1. Open Create Saved Response popup
2. Click Cancel button / X button
3. Verify popup
Title: Cancel / X closes popup
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Create Saved Response popup

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click 3-dot button on a response
3. Click Edit response
4. Verify popup
Title: Edit Saved Response popup opens
Expectation: - SHOULD display Edit Saved Response popup with title "Edit Saved Response"

Step:
1. Open Edit Saved Response popup
2. Verify pre-populated fields
Title: Fields pre-populated with existing data
Expectation:
- SHOULD pre-populate TITLE field with existing title
- SHOULD pre-populate MESSAGE field with existing message

Step:
1. Make no changes in Edit popup
2. Click X button
3. Verify popup
Title: X closes popup without changes
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Edit Saved Response popup

Step:
1. Make a change in Edit popup
2. Click X button
3. Verify Discard Changes popup
Title: X with changes shows Discard Changes popup
Expectation:
- SHOULD show Discard Changes popup with title "Discard Changes?"
- SHOULD show text: "Are you sure you want to go? Changes have not been saved yet."

Step:
1. Discard Changes popup is shown
2. Click Cancel button or X icon
3. Verify popup
Title: Cancel / X on Discard Changes closes it
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Discard Changes popup and return to Edit popup

Step:
1. Discard Changes popup is shown
2. Click Discard Changes button
3. Verify behavior
Title: Discard Changes discards edits
Expectation: - SHOULD close Discard Changes popup and Edit popup without saving changes

Step:
1. Make no changes in Edit popup
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify popup
Title: Cancel closes popup without changes
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Edit Saved Response popup

Step:
1. Make a change in Edit popup
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify Discard Changes popup
Title: Cancel with changes shows Discard Changes popup
Expectation: - SHOULD show Discard Changes popup (same as X button behavior)

Step:
1. Edit both fields
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save updates response with toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD save new changes for the response
- SHOULD display toast: "Saved Response updated successfully."

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click 3-dot button on a response
3. Click Delete response
4. Verify confirmation popup
Title: Delete confirmation popup shown
Expectation:
- SHOULD display confirmation popup with title "Delete Response?" - SHOULD display text: "This response will be permanently deleted and can't be recovered.
Are you sure you want to proceed?"

Step:
1. Delete confirmation popup is shown
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify popup
Title: Cancel closes delete popup
Expectation: - SHOULD close the confirmation popup without deleting

Step:
1. Delete confirmation popup is shown
2. Click OK button
3. Verify result
Title: OK deletes response with toast
Expectation: - SHOULD close the popup, delete the response, and display toast: "Saved Response deleted."

AC: AC8: Add saved response to chat box
Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click title of a saved response item / press Enter
3. Verify chatbox
Title: Click title / Enter appends message to chatbox
Expectation: - SHOULD append the full message content into the chatbox, placed behind current text (if any)

US: US2: Create a saved response from a message in the conversation
AC: AC1: Add Save this response button to Coach's non-text message
Step:
1. Login as Coach (Pro/Studio plan workspace)
2. Open a conversation
3. Hover over a non-text message of the Coach
4. Verify bookmark button
Title: Bookmark button shown on hover (paid plan)
Expectation: - SHOULD display a bookmark button between 3-dot and Reply buttons for the non-text message

Step:
1. Hover over the bookmark button
2. Verify tooltip
Title: Bookmark button tooltip
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip: "Saved this response"

Step:
1. Login as Coach (free/no Saved Response plan workspace)
2. Hover over a non-text message of the Coach
3. Verify bookmark button
Title: Bookmark button hidden on non-qualifying plan
Expectation: - SHOULD NOT display bookmark button when workspace does not include Saved Response feature

AC: AC2: Add new saved response from the conversation
Step:
1. Click bookmark button on a Coach message
2. Verify popup
Title: Add To Saved Responses popup opens
Expectation: - SHOULD display the Create Saved Response popup with title "Add To Saved Responses"

Step:
1. Open Add To Saved Responses popup
2. Verify pre-populated fields
Title: Title blank, Message pre-populated with coach message
Expectation:
- SHOULD leave Title field blank
- SHOULD pre-populate Message field with the Coach's message

Step:
1. Coach message is over 10,000 characters
2. Click bookmark button
3. Verify Message field
Title: Coach message over 10,000 chars truncated
Expectation: - SHOULD populate Message field with first 10,000 characters of the Coach's message

Step:
1. Fill Title field
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save adds response with toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD add the message as a new saved response
- SHOULD display toast: "Response has been saved"
